one_time_description: Specific claims revenue of $403,650 recognized in the year
economic_notes: Multiple government business enterprises including LBB Gas Bar & Convenience LP, LBB Coffee Franchise LP, Wakayos Developments LP, Wakayos Holdings Inc., Wakayos Employment & Training Center Inc., DS-LBB Holdings Inc. Also holds 8.3% interest in Keseechiwan Holdings Limited Partnership. URSA Cannabis and Holistic Health Ltd. is fully consolidated.
notes: Little Black Bear First Nation is located in Saskatchewan. URSA Cannabis and Holistic Health Ltd. is the one fully consolidated subsidiary. Nine GBEs accounted for under modified equity method including gas bar, coffee franchise, and development entities. Net debt position of $11.3M. Significant specific claims revenue of $403,650 recognized as likely one-time item. Fisheries and Oceans Canada revenue of $591,520 appears new in 2024 (nil in 2023). Large increase in accounts receivable from $40,610 to $715,245 primarily due to specific claims receivable. Reserve for losses in GBEs of $2.29M reflects ongoing losses in gas bar and coffee franchise operations. Current portion of long-term debt increased significantly from $584,618 to $2,226,695.
